pattern: Greedy two-pointer: assign the current minimum available value when the instruction is 'I' and the current maximum when it is 'D', then place the remaining value last.
hints: Maintain two pointers, lo=0 and hi=n. For each character in s, choose greedily: 'I' wants the smallest remaining value (pick lo++), 'D' wants the largest (pick hi--).
After processing all n characters of s you have one value left (lo == hi); place it at the end.
This greedy always produces a valid permutation because each 'I' gets a value smaller than all future 'D'-picked values, and vice versa.

edge_inputs.description: DDI at the end switches direction
